title: oxalis phaeotricha var. glandulosa
intro: Oxalis phaeotricha var. glandulosa is a rare and lesser-known variety of the wood sorrel plant, belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Oxalis phaeotricha var. glandulosa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
